title: stylidium piliferum subsp. minor
intro: Stylidium piliferum subsp. minor, also known as the Kidney Stylidium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Stylidiaceae, commonly found in Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits associated with Stylidium piliferum subsp. minor include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to create a tonic or salve, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
